question: A vendor sells shirts at Rs500 each, earning a 2% commission, and ties at Rs150 each, with a 12% commission. If he sells 3 shirts and 4 ties per day, what will be his total commission earned in 30 days?
A fruit seller sells apples at a loss of 5% on the cost price but uses a weight that is 20% less than the actual weight. Find his total profit percentage.
The profit made on an item sold for Rs2200 is equal to the loss incurred when it is sold for Rs1800. What will be the profit or loss percentage if the item is sold for Rs2050?
A vendor sold 15 toys for Rs3000 and gained a profit of 25%. How many toys should he sell for Rs2816 to make a profit of 10%?

An antique dealer sold three items, A, B, and C, with their selling prices in the ratio 4:5:6. He suffered a loss of 10% on item A, made a profit of 50% on item B, and broke even (no profit, no loss) on item C. What was his approximate percentage gain or loss in the overall deal?
A shopkeeper made a loss of Rs125 on an article. If the loss percentage was 5%, what was the cost price of the article (in Rs)?

A clothing store sold three dresses, D1, D2, and D3, whose selling prices were in the ratio 5:6:7. They made a profit of 20% on D1, a loss of 10% on D2, and a profit of 30% on D3. What was their approximate total profit or loss percentage for the entire sale?
A bookseller sells 10 pens for Rs300, but in doing so, he incurs a loss equal to the cost price of 4 pens. The loss percent is:
A flower vendor bought 200 flowers for Rs800. 20 flowers withered and became unsellable. At what price per flower should he sell the remaining flowers to earn a profit of 30% on his total cost?
The ratio of the profit to the cost price of a product is 2: 7. What is the ratio of its selling price to its profit?
A sweet shop owner buys 10 boxes of sweets for the price of 8 boxes from a wholesaler. If he sells each box at a 2% discount on its marked price, what is his profit percentage?

A seller bought three different types of mobile covers, C1, C2, and C3. The ratio of their selling prices was 3:4:5. He made a profit of 25% on C1, 10% on C2, but incurred a loss of 20% on C3. What was his approximate percent gain or loss in the entire transaction?

A grocer purchased 50 kg of rice at Rs40 per kg and 20 kg of another variety of rice at Rs55 per kg. He mixed the two varieties and sold the entire mixture at Rs48 per kg. Find his total profit or loss in this transaction.
If the ratio between the loss and cost price of an article is 1 : 10, then the ratio between its selling price and cost price is ____.

A shopkeeper mixes two varieties of pulses - one costing Rs80 per kg and the other costing Rs100 per kg, in the ratio 3 : 1. If he sells the mixed variety at Rs91.80 per kg, find his gain or loss percent.
A fruit vendor sells 15 kg of apples for Rs750, thereby gaining the cost price of 5 kg of apples. What is his profit percentage?
A vendor purchases 7 shirts at the marked price of 6 shirts. If he sells each shirt after giving a 4% discount on its marked price, what is his profit percentage?

A bookseller purchases 5 novels at the marked price of 4 novels from a distributor. If he then sells these novels to customers, offering a discount of 5% on the marked price, what is his profit percentage?
A grocer mixes two varieties of rice - one costing Rs40 per kg and the other costing Rs60 per kg, in the ratio 2 : 3. If he sells the mixed variety at Rs57.20 per kg, find his gain or loss percent.

A can contains 40 litres of oil at Rs. 50/litre. Another can contains 60 litres of oil at Rs. 60/litre. They are mixed, and 20 litres are sold at Rs. 70/litre. How much profit or loss does the seller make on this sale?
A businessman invests Rs. 50,000 in buying items and spends Rs. 5,000 on transportation and Rs. 3,000 on storage. If he sells the items for Rs. 63,000, what is the profit or loss percentage?

A person invested in two items — one gave a 10% profit and the other a 5% loss. If the total investment was Rs. 5,000 and the net result was a Rs. 100 profit, what was the investment in the profit-making item?
A man buys a set of 3 identical watches at a total cost of Rs. 4,500. He sells them at Rs. 1,800, Rs. 1,700, and Rs. 1,600, respectively. What is the overall profit or loss?

An electronics store orders 150 portable speakers at a cost of Rs400 each. The owner plans to price them so that selling only 120 speakers would guarantee a 40% profit on the total investment. However, a local event leads to the sale of 135 speakers. If the remaining speakers have no resale value, calculate the store's actual profit percentage on the total investment.
If the selling price of an item is increased by 25% and the profit also increases from 20% to 30%, what is the percentage increase in the cost price?
A shopkeeper bought 100 pens at Rs 10 each. He sold 60 pens at 20% profit and the rest at 10% loss. What is the overall profit or loss percentage?
A shopkeeper sells two shirts at Rs. 500 each. On one, he gains 25%, and on the other, he loses 25%. What is his net gain or loss?
If the selling price of an article is 90% of its marked price and the cost price is 80% of the marked price, what is the profit percentage ?
A man buys 8 shirts for Rs. 4,000 and sells 6 of them for Rs. 3,900. If he discards the remaining 2 shirts, what is his overall gain or loss?
The ratio of the profit to the cost price is 3:8. What is the ratio of the selling price to the cost price? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 3]

A person sells a product at a profit of 15%. If he had bought it for 10% less and sold it for Rs18 less, he would have gained 25%. Find the cost price of the product. [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 13 Nov Shift 1]

A trader buys 50 kg of rice at Rs50 per kg and 30 kg of a different variety at Rs60 per kg. He mixes the two varieties and sells the entire mixture at Rs55 per kg. What is his total profit or loss? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 13 Nov 2025, Shift-2]
A bicycle is sold for Rs1800 at a loss of 10%. At what price should it be sold to gain 15%?

A trader buys 50 kg of rice at Rs50 per kg and 30 kg of a different variety at Rs60 per kg. He mixes the two varieties and sells the entire mixture at Rs55 per kg. What is his total profit or loss? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 13 Nov 2025, Shift-2]

If equal quantities of two types of sugar are mixed, where the first is sold for Rs40/kg at a 25% profit, and the second is sold for Rs30/kg at a 25% loss. If the mixture is sold at Rs36/kg, what is the loss or gain percentage?
A person sells 12 pens for Rs480 and suffers a loss equal to the selling price of 3 pens. What is the cost price of one pen?

A dealer bought two antique items. On the first item, he gained 25%, and on the second, he lost 10%. The cost price of the first item was 60% more than the second. If the overall profit from both items was Rs225, find the cost price of each item. [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-1]

A dealer buys a batch of 25 pressure cookers at Rs800 each. He sells 15 of them at 20% profit and the remaining at 10% loss. Find the overall profit or loss percentage. [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-1]

A trader buys 200 mixed stationery boxes at a rate of Rs120 each. He spends Rs1,800 on transportation and packaging. He sells 100 boxes at Rs150 each and the remaining at Rs130 each. What is his total profit percentage? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-2]

A glass manufacturer produces transparent right prism blocks with a square base of side 10 cm and height 25 cm. If the selling price is Rs0.60 per cm³ and the cost price is Rs0.40 per cm³, what is the total profit earned on selling 12 such prisms? [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-3]
A man purchased 3 identical electric fans at a total cost of Rs4,320. He sold two of them at a gain of 15% each and the third one at a loss of 10%. What is his overall profit or loss percentage?
A trader sells two articles at Rs960 each. On one, he gains 20%, and on the other, he loses 20%. What is his overall profit or loss percentage?
Ravi sold a bicycle for Rs4200 at a 5% profit. What should be the selling price of the bicycle to gain a 15% profit?
A man bought a watch for Rs1500 and sold it at a loss of 8%. What is the selling price?
A person sells two products for Rs500 each. He makes a 20% profit on one and incurs a 20% loss on the other. What is his net gain or loss on the whole transaction?
An item was sold for Rs12000 at a 20% loss. At what price should it be sold to gain a 20% profit?
A shopkeeper sells 15 kg of sugar for Rs450 and earns a profit equal to the selling price of 3 kg of sugar. What is the profit percentage?
A person sells two items for Rs200 each. He gains 25% on one and loses 25% on the other. What is his total loss percentage in the entire transaction?

One article is sold at 10% profit and another (different article) is sold at 20% loss. Determine the ratio of their cost prices so that the seller breaks even (overall no profit, no loss) on the two articles combined.
A car was sold for Rs3,30,000 at a 10% profit. For what price should the car be sold to gain a 25% profit?
A man purchases a certain number of identical calculators at a rate of Rs1,200 per dozen. He sells them at Rs110 per piece. Find how many calculators he bought if his total profit is Rs360.
A shopkeeper buys an article for Rs. 100 and sells it for Rs. 120. What is the profit percentage?
An item bought for Rs. 250 is sold at Rs. 300. Find the profit percentage.

A bookstore bought 120 different novels at an average price of Rs145. It sold 40 novels at 20% profit, 60 novels at 10% loss, and the rest were sold in a combo deal that included a 30% profit on the total cost of those remaining novels. What was the selling price per novel in the combo deal?

Manu printed 20 books of 200 pages each. The cost of printing is Rs. 25 per page. After this, he spent Rs. 80 per book on binding. After this, he spent Rs. 2,000 on advertising the books and Rs. 50 per book in supply. If at the end he sold each book for Rs. 7500, then find the amount gained (profit) by Manu.
By selling 15 articles, a man gains the selling price of 3 articles. Find the profit percentage.

A trader bought 120 scientific calculators, of which 50 were priced at Rs625.50 each, and the rest at Rs712.80 each. Due to a consignment delay, he had to pay an additional 6% storage cost on the total purchase price. He sold 40 of the cheaper ones at a profit of 15%, and the rest at a loss of 10%. All costlier calculators were sold at a 12% profit. What was his overall profit or loss percentage?
A man bought a pen for Rs. 45 and sold it for Rs. 60. What is the profit percent?
A trader sells an article to A at 20% profit. A then sells to B at 10% loss. B sells to C at 5% profit. If C pays Rs1,134, what was the original cost price of the article (the trader’s CP)?
A man sold an article at a profit of 25%. Had he sold it for Rs. 60 more, he would have gained 40%. Find the cost price.
A vendor sells an item at 10% loss. If he increases the selling price by Rs. 45, he would have made a 5% gain. Find the cost price.
A dishonest dealer professes to sell goods at cost price but uses a false weight of 950g instead of 1kg. Find his profit percentage.
If profit earned is 25% of the selling price, what is the profit percentage on cost price?
On selling 17 articles, a trader gains the cost price of 3 articles. What is his profit percentage?

A trader bought 300 kg of rice, part at Rs18 per kg and the rest at Rs24 per kg. He sold all at Rs27 per kg and made a total profit of Rs2,100. If the quantity bought at Rs18 was twice that bought at Rs24, find the quantity bought at Rs24 per kg.
Profit of Rs. 25 is made on an article costing Rs. 125. What is the profit percentage?
A shopkeeper sells a product at Rs. 80 after losing 20%. What is the cost price?
If an article is sold for Rs. 96 at a loss of 20%, what was its cost price?
Loss of Rs. 15 is incurred on selling an article for Rs. 135. What was the cost price?
A shopkeeper makes a 20% profit on an article whose cost price is 250. What is the selling price of the article?
A grocer sells sugar at a 10% loss. Had he sold it for Rs0.60 more per kg, he would have gained 8%. What is the cost price of sugar per kg?
An article costing Rs. 200 is sold at a loss of 10%. Find the selling price.
If the selling price of 15 items is equal to the cost price of 20 items, what is the percentage profit or loss?
A trader sold an item at a profit of 20%. If the cost price was Rs. 500, find the selling price.
A fruit vendor sold 3/4 of his apples at a profit of 20% and the rest at a loss of 10%. If he gained Rs90 on the whole, find the cost price of all apples.
A vendor buys apples at 10 for Rs. 120 and sells them at 8 for Rs. 100. What is his profit or loss percentage? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 2]
A shopkeeper bought 100 pens at Rs 10 each. He sold 60 pens at 20% profit and the rest at 10% loss. What is the overall profit or loss percentage?
An art dealer sells a painting for Rs. 6,600, making a profit of 10% after paying Rs. 600 in restoration charges. What was the original purchase price?

A trader purchases 100 smartphones at Rs. 12,000 each. He spends Rs. 30,000 on transportation and Rs. 20,000 on advertising. 10 phones are damaged in transit and sold at 50% loss. The remaining are sold at a 25% profit. What is his net profit or loss?
If the selling price of an item is increased by 25% and the profit also increases from 20% to 30%, what is the percentage increase in the cost price?
An item is sold at a 20% profit. If the cost price and selling price are increased by Rs. 50 and Rs. 30 respectively, the profit percent becomes 10%. What is the original cost price?
An article bought for Rs150 is sold for Rs135. Find the loss percentage.
A shopkeeper bought an article for Rs2500 and sold it at a profit of 12%. What is the selling price?
A shopkeeper buys an article for Rs500 and sells it for Rs600. What is the profit percentage?
A trader sells an article at 15% profit. Had he sold it for Rs150 less, he would have incurred a 5% loss. Find the cost price.
A man bought 30 pens at Rs12 each and 20 pens at Rs15 each. He sold all pens at Rs18 each. What is his total profit?
A product is sold at 25% profit. If the cost price is Rs240, find the selling price.
A person bought an item for Rs1200 and sold it for Rs1320. Find the profit percentage.

An event management team is organizing a local film festival in a community hall. The total cost to host the event, including venue rent, equipment, and promotions, is Rs10,000. They plan to recover this amount by selling entry tickets, each priced at Rs200.How many tickets must be sold for the organizers to break even, i.e., recover the entire event cost without making a profit or a loss?
Startup buys a table at Rs5,000, spends Rs2,000 refurbishing it, and sells it for Rs9,000. Find profit or loss %.
A combo set (mug + coffee beans) is sold at Rs600. The mug costs Rs250, beans Rs280. Find profit or loss %.
A trader bought goods at and made a profit of . Find the profit percentage.
A trader sold an article for Rs720 at a 20% loss. What was the cost price?
A student bought a calculator for Rs400 and sold it for Rs440. What is the profit percentage?
A trader sold an article for Rs720 at a 20% gain. Find the cost price.
A trader purchased goods for Rs. 1200 and sold them for Rs. 1440. What is the profit percentage?
An article was sold for Rs. 990 at a 10% loss. What was its cost price?
An article was sold for Rs 1350 at a profit of 12.5%. What was its cost price?
Aman purchased goods worth Rs. 800 and sold them at a 25% profit. What was the selling price?
A trader buys a bag for Rs. 200 and sells it for Rs. 250. What is the profit percentage?
Aman bought goods for Rs. 1200 and sold them at a 15% loss. What was the selling price?

A trader bought goods for 240 and sold them at a 25% profit. What was the selling price?
A book was purchased for Rs350 and sold with a profit of Rs70. What is the profit percentage?

A shopkeeper buys an item for 250 and earns 20% profit. What is the selling price?

A man bought a laptop for Rs1600 but sold it for Rs1520. Find the percentage loss.
A trader bought goods for Rs500 and sold them at a gain of 20%. What was the selling price?
A product was bought for Rs 500 and sold at a 15% loss. What is the selling price?

: 1000 800 ? एक वस्तु का विक्रय मूल्य तथा क्रय मूल्य क्रमश रुपये तथा रुपये है। लाभ प्रतिशत क्या है En: Selling price and cost price of an article is Rs. 1000 and Rs. 800 respectively. What is the profit percentage? प्रतिशत प्रतिशत प्रतिशत प्रतिशत

2000 , 1200 19 किसी वस्तु को रुपये में बेचने पर हुआ लाभ उसी वस्तु को रुपये में बेचने पर हुई हानि का गुना है। 2000 ? रुपये के विक्रय मूल्य पर इस वस्तु का लाभ प्रतिशत क्या है En: The profit earned on selling an article for Rs. 2000 is 19 times of the loss incurred on selling the same article for Rs. 1200. Ratio of the cost price of an article to the selling price of the article is 10: 13. What will be the ratio of the value of profit to the cost price of the article?

Selling price of an article is Rs. 3500. If the value of the loss is Rs. 500, then what is the loss percentage?
The difference between cost price and selling price of an article is Rs. 385. Profit percentage is 7 percent. What is the cost price of the article?
Selling price of an article is Rs. 2300. If the value of the loss is Rs. 200, then what is the loss percentage?

The selling price of an item is Rs.4200. If the profit value is Rs.200, then what will be the profit percentage?
5 times of selling price of an article is equal to 4 times of cost price of the article. What is the loss percentage?

The cost price of 15 articles is Rs.182. The selling price of 20 articles is Rs.182. What is the ratio of the value of profit/loss on 1 article to the cost price of 1 article? (The cost price of all articles is same. The selling price of all articles is same.)
Selling price of an article is Rs. 4400. If the value of profit is Rs. 400, then what is the profit percentage?
Cost price of an article is Rs. 480. The profit percentage is 10 percent. What is the value of profit?
A person has a loss of 25 percent on selling price. What will be his loss percentage on the cost price?

40 percent of the cost price of an article is equal to 50 percent of its selling price. What is the loss percentage?
A person earns 30 percent profit on selling price. What will be his profit percentage on the cost price?

Selling price and cost price of an article is Rs. 550 and Rs. 500 respectively. What is the profit percentage?
A person earns a loss of 30 percent on selling price. What will be his loss percentage on the cost price?
A person sells an item at a profit of 30 percent. Both the cost price and selling price are reduced by 40 percent. What will be the new profit percentage?

The cost price of 30 items is Rs.260. The selling price of 40 items is Rs.260. What is the profit/loss percentage? (The cost price of all items is the same. The selling price of all items is the same.)
A trader sells two articles at the same selling price. On one, he makes 10% profit, on the other, he incurs 10% loss. What is his overall profit or loss on the entire transaction?

A dealer professes to sell goods at a loss of 10% but uses a weight of 750 g instead of 1 kg. What is his real gain or loss percentage?

A dealer makes a profit equal to the selling price of 4 pens when he sells a total of 12 pens. What is his profit percentage?

300 kg of rice was purchased at the rate of Rs20 per kg and sold at a profit of 5%. Compute the profit and the selling price per kg.
An article is sold for a loss of Rs. 120. Loss percentage is 40 percent. What is the cost price of the article?

An article is sold for a loss of 40 percent. What is the ratio of the selling price of the article to the cost price of the article?
